title: CDX Conformance Fixture Case (Level 1)
description: NON-NORMATIVE. A Level-1 document fixture is a directory under conformance/fixtures/<kind>/<case>/ containing case.json (this schema) and case.cdx (the committed byte-stable archive, which MUST equal buildZip(case.json.recipe) — enforced by check:fixtures). case.json carries the deterministic archive build spec (`recipe`, interpreted by scripts/lib/zip-writer.ts) and the expected verdict (`expect`). The verdict is an interval over the disposition lattice (State Machine 07 section 5.4.1): IGNORE < WARNING < INTEGRITY-ERROR < REJECT. The fixture corpus is generated from scripts/lib/fixture-corpus.ts (the reviewed source of truth) and re-verified against it by the gate, mirroring the kat -> vectors pattern.

interval.description: An inclusive [atLeast, atMost] band over the lattice. INTEGRITY-ERROR is a floor with MAY-escalation (section 5.4.1), so a defect whose spec disposition is INTEGRITY-ERROR is authored [INTEGRITY-ERROR, REJECT]; an exact REJECT is [REJECT, REJECT]; a flat IGNORE is [IGNORE, IGNORE]. The atMost ceiling is where the suite's teeth are.

code.description: A conformance/errors.json defect code. Its disposition is authoritative in errors.json; the interval here is the per-finding expectation.

findings.description: The intended findings, checked as a SUBSET of what the adapter reports: a correct implementation MAY report additional findings, but MUST report at least these, each within its interval.

layer.description: The reader layer this case exercises. B1a asserts only `container`; `document` (part loader) arrives in B1b.

requires.description: Capability keys (conformance/capabilities.json) this case needs; a case is scoped out of an adapter that lacks one.
